title: Historic-sourcing backlog
description: The PROD-14 sourcing backlog: a pool of candidate targets for the historic-sourcing track. Each target names a flagship product, its anchor category, a documented driver, a tier hint and a one-line editorial rationale. Targets are CANDIDATES the SPIKE-01 spike winnows, not confirmed Tier A. Selection is editorial by named criteria (see docs/SELECTION-CRITERIA.md), never a computed transformation metric.

note.description: Optional plain-English note about the backlog and its candidate framing.

targets.description: At least 20 candidate targets across tiers A/B/C. The pool must not be all-Tier-A-candidate, so it cannot over-promise against the project's roughly 15 to 20 Tier A feasibility ceiling.

name.description: The candidate product or product family.

category.description: One of the flagship anchor categories.

driver.description: The documented driver this target is anchored to.

type.description: A mandate compels a compositional change; an incentive only nudges (a levy or a voluntary programme).

name.description: Name of the documented driver, for example the 2018 Soft Drinks Industry Levy or the FSA sugar reduction programme.

tier.description: Tier hint. A-candidate: a sourceable historical formulation looks likely. B-candidate: category-level reformulation is documentable. C-candidate: current-only, included for category breadth.

rationale.description: One-line editorial rationale for including this target, by named criteria, never a computed score.
